name: Fish Slices, Peking Style
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------MARINADE-------------------------
1/2 teaspoon Salt
1/8 teaspoon White pepper
1/4 teaspoon Sugar
2 tablespoons Dry sherry
1 tablespoon Freshly minced ginger
1 Beaten egg
---------------------------SAUCE---------------------------
1 tablespoon White vinegar
4 teaspoons Sugar
3 tablespoons Black soy sauce
2 tablespoons Rice wine
1 cup Chicken broth
2 tablespoons Cornstarch mixed with 3 tablespoons water
1 teaspoon Sesame oil
1. Mix marinade ingredients and marinate fish slices for 30 minutes.
2. Place dried mushrooms in warm water for 15+ minutes to soften.
3. Dredge marinated fish slices in cornstarch while heating oil in a wok.
4. Deep fry fish slices until light brown, then drain on paper towels.
5. Remove all but 2 tablespoons of oil from the wok. Stir-fry garlic, chilies, ginger, and scallions.
6. Add mushrooms, stir in sauce mixture until it boils, then add cornstarch mixture and cook until thickened.
7. Return fish slices to the wok, drizzle with sesame seed oil.
8. Eat!
